title: bossiaea aquifolium
intro: Bossiaea aquifolium, also known as the holly-leaved bossiaea, is a flowering plant native to Australia, belonging to the family Polygalaceae. It has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Bossiaea aquifolium include its potential to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy as a treatment.
